domain: Inuit ethnoscience (sea ice taxonomy, e.g. Iñupiaq Qanuq Ilitaavut, Inuktitut siku terminology)
specific_mechanism: Indigenous Arctic sea-ice vocabularies encode 30-100+ discrete categorical labels for ice states (slush, frazil, drift, multi-year, rotten, pressure-ridge, fast-ice, lead-ice, etc.) selected because each distinction is operationally consequential for travel/hunting safety. The taxonomy is purpose-built, learned through deliberate exposure to canonical examples, and includes an open-class mechanism for new state types not previously named.
llm_application: Build domain-specific high-resolution operational-state taxonomies (20-50 categories per deployment) for high-stakes LLM agents (manufacturing-line, clinical decision support, security ops), elicited through expert annotation + agent self-supervised category discovery + open-class novel-state handling. Distinct from generic confidence/uncertainty scalars by giving operators by-type labels to act on.
novelty_claim: Toward Culturally Aligned LLMs (2601.21700) created 76 fine-grained value categories for ontology-guided LLM agent reasoning — directly the fine-grained-taxonomy-for-LLM-agent approach. Ontology learning for LLMs (2508.19428, 2312.00326, 2602.01276, 2604.00555) covers term extraction, ontology matching, construction. The Inuit-ice-as-inspiration framing is not directly imported, but the fine-grained-LLM-agent-ontology approach is published.
